title: Veggie-Loaded Crustless Quiche
description: A flexible, protein-rich crustless quiche built around whatever vegetables you have. Works for breakfast, brunch, or a light dinner. Easy to batch and reheat.

ingredients.name: shredded cheese (cheddar, Swiss, Gruyère, or a mix)
chopped vegetables (broccoli, spinach, bell pepper, zucchini, mushrooms, asparagus — lightly cooked first)
fresh herbs (parsley, chives, basil, or thyme), chopped

instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F. Grease a 9-inch pie dish or 8x8-inch baking dish.

Heat olive oil or butter in a skillet over medium heat. Add onion and cook until softened, 3–4 minutes. Add garlic and remaining vegetables and sauté until just tender, 5–7 minutes. Season lightly with salt and pepper. If using spinach, add it last so it just wilts.
In a large bowl, whisk together eggs, milk, half-and-half (if using), salt, pepper, and paprika.
Spread cooked vegetables evenly in the dish. Sprinkle cheese over them. Pour egg mixture on top. Finish with fresh herbs.
Bake 30–35 minutes until the center is set and the top is lightly golden. Rest 5–10 minutes before slicing — this helps it firm up.
notes:
Variations:
• Mediterranean: spinach, sun-dried tomatoes, artichoke hearts, feta, oregano
• Southwest: bell peppers, onions, black beans, corn, pepper jack, cumin
• Garden Fresh: zucchini, cherry tomatoes, basil, mozzarella

Reheats well — good candidate for batch cooking. Slice and refrigerate for up to 4 days.
